resolution_notes: Cascade science verified. H(r) numerics independently confirmed: H(7400)=3618 km (Cancer), H(12600)=1979 km (Capricorn), 82.8% seasonal swing — all match section claims. Key logical chain holds: (1) sun at 5,733 km exceeds firmament ceiling at ALL orbital radii (highest=3,618 km < 5,733 km); (2) physical/apparent split makes physical altitude vary 1,975–3,618 km seasonally; (3) physical-position predictions become indeterminate. Verdict changes scientifically justified: WIN-044 (Misleading→Self-Contradicted) — FSF derivation uses H(r) which the dome's own fix makes seasonally indeterminate, a genuine self-contradiction not just misleading framing. WIN-068/WIN-010 (Std Model Explains→Self-Contradicted) — eclipse shadow geometry requires a definite physical sun altitude; the cascade makes it indeterminate across all 39 eclipses. Self-Contradicted is the stronger primary verdict because it shows the dome cannot generate a consistent prediction regardless of whether Chapman ionospheric mechanism also explains the data. Chapman retained as secondary. The other-side paragraph correctly identifies the strongest counter (apparent-position predictions unaffected by construction) and correctly scopes the cascade to physical-position dependencies only. Recommend human approval of all three verdict changes.

resolved_reason: WIN-021 ISS-1842/1843 substantive review: BOTH PATCHES VERIFIED. (1) Duplicate-sentence dedup: detail_evidence now contains "multi-domain automated audit engine" exactly once (39-domain duplicate copy removed); reads cleanly. (2) Escape-hatch preemption: "A defender might argue the rad/s² is intentional (predicting precession acceleration, not rate) — but VLBI and lunar laser ranging measure precession rate as effectively constant on human timescales, so a specific angular-acceleration value would imply a detectable changing precession rate that neither historical data nor modern geodesy records." Sentence is well-placed (between dimensional-error setup and 37%-magnitude-mismatch fallback) and converts the intentional-rad/s² defense into a falsifiable empirical claim that VLBI/LLR refute. Three-layer argument structure intact: (a) dimensional error rad/s² vs rad/s, (b) magnitude 37% off even after charitable unit correction (4.87×10⁻¹² vs 7.73×10⁻¹² — verified arithmetic: 1−4.87/7.73 = 0.370), (c) circular derivation from assumed dome cavity parameters, no first-principles mechanism. Verdict "Misleading" still well-supported. Prior analysis stands; no new expansion needed.
